title: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : Central District Council (cemetery) Bye-laws
title_en: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : Central District Council (cemetery) Bye-laws

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content:
1. Citation
These Bye-Laws may be cited as the Central District Council (Cemetery) Bye-Laws*.
*Originally made under the Township Act now repealed, these regulations have been continued under s 94(2) of the Local Government Act, 2013.

provisions.title: Interpretation
Establishment
provisions.content:
3. Establishment
The Council shall establish, designate and identify areas within the jurisdiction of the Council as cemeteries.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content:
4. Application
These Bye-Laws are applicable to all village areas under the jurisdiction of the Council.

provisions.title: Burials in cemetery
provisions.content:
5. Burials in cemetery
No person shall bury or cause to be buried, a body in a place other than in a cemetery.

definitions.definition: the Council Secretary or any employee of the Council duly authorised
the person designated as such by the Council and employed under the Unified Local Government Service Act (Cap. 40:06)
any designated area established as a cemetery under bye-law 3
a person below the age of 10 years
the area of jurisdiction of the Central District Council
any gravestone, monument epitaph, tablet or monumental inscription and any work ancillary thereto
any village area under the Central District Council.
